title: setting max_final_threads=1 makes queries significantly faster. Something wrong?
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the situation
We recently upgraded from clickhouse 23.12 to 25.5, and while stuff was in general faster, we found that setting max_final_threads=1 would in general make our queries much faster. E.g. from three seconds to half a second! This was not previously the case, in older versions of clickhouse, the default value of this setting works much better. So question is, if there is a bug, if the default value might need to be changed, or if it is just our specific usecases that has this curious behavior.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
25.7.4 and 25.5.1, and probably more versions.
### How to reproduce
A minimal example to reproduce (both on 25.7.4 and 25.5.1)
```
-- 1. Drop old test table if exists
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS test_final_partitions;
-- 2. Create a simplified ReplacingMergeTree with timestamp partitioning
CREATE TABLE test_final_partitions
(
organization_id UInt32,
keyword_id UInt32,
search_date DateTime,
position_on_serp UInt32,
version UInt32,
value String
)
ENGINE = ReplacingMergeTree(version)
PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(search_date)
ORDER BY (organization_id, keyword_id, search_date, position_on_serp);
-- 3. Insert synthetic data across multiple months/partitions
--    Let's make ~50M rows across 6 months
INSERT INTO test_final_partitions
SELECT
rand() % 1000 AS organization_id,
rand() % 100000 AS keyword_id,
toDateTime('2023-01-01') + (rand() % (86400*180)) AS search_date,  -- random over ~6 months
rand() % 50 AS position_on_serp,
number % 5 AS version,   -- multiple versions per row
toString(number)
FROM numbers(50000000);
```
Then run
`clickhouse-benchmark --query "SELECT count() FROM test_final_partitions FINAL group by search_date"`
And
`clickhouse-benchmark --query "SELECT count() FROM test_final_partitions FINAL group by search_date max_final_threads=1"`
### Expected performance
I was expecting the default setting for max_final_threads to provide better results. I have tried several different machines with various number of cores, and in general using one thread is faster.
### Additional context
_No response_

key_bug_features: Setting max_final_threads=1 improves query performance significantly
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_final_partitions (organization_id UInt32, keyword_id UInt32, search_date DateTime, position_on_serp UInt32, version UInt32, value String) ENGINE = ReplacingMergeTree(version) PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(search_date) ORDER BY (organization_id, keyword_id, search_date, position_on_serp);

INSERT INTO test_final_partitions SELECT rand() % 1000 AS organization_id, rand() % 100000 AS keyword_id, toDateTime('2023-01-01') + (rand() % (86400*180)) AS search_date, rand() % 50 AS position_on_serp, number % 5 AS version, toString(number) FROM numbers(50000000);
SELECT count() FROM test_final_partitions FINAL WHERE organization_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY search_date;
SELECT count() FROM test_final_partitions FINAL WHERE position_on_serp > 25 AND version IS NULL GROUP BY search_date;
